notes.resolve: Lite & Verified resolve % are REAL, from swe-bench/experiments (results/results.json, resolved/denom). Pro % are from the Scale SEAL public standardized set (morphllm.com/swe-bench-pro). Darwin entries are our own gold-graded runs on the official swebench harness.
notes.cost: Official entries' cost/instance is ESTIMATED from each system's DISCLOSED model(s) + scaffold shape × public token pricing — not a figure those teams published; order-of-magnitude only (marked 'est'). 'undisclosed' = no public model/cost. Darwin costs are MEASURED from actual OpenRouter spend.
notes.darwin: Darwin is measured on SWE-bench Lite (conformant: solver never sees gold tests in-loop). Verified/Pro runs are not done yet — those boards are shown for competitive context.

entries.name: Darwin · single-traj
entries.note: 102/300 full-300, gold, conformant; cost = measured

entries.note: 119/300 full-300 judge-only (measured); union ceiling 45%; env-filter (faster impl) is the lever toward 45%

entries.name: Darwin · GLM-5.2 single

entries.name: Darwin · GLM→Opus empty-patch cascade
entries.scaffold: cheap solve → escalate EMPTY patches to Opus (100%-precision gate)
entries.note: 154/300 full-300 gold, conformant (§28); 187 GLM non-empty + 113 Opus-escalated empties; blended measured cost. Breaks 50%, clears the ~45% cheap-union ceiling.

draco.label: DRACO — Deep Research (quality)
draco.metricNote: Different metric: 'Resolve %' here = DRACO research-quality × 100 (0–100), NOT bug-resolve. 'Cost' is per full corpus run (MEASURED, not estimated); Run total = that run cost.
draco.darwinNote: DRACO is our own cross-domain research benchmark. The cheap model beats the frontier on BOTH quality and cost — the harness/cost-router is the lever. All numbers measured (qualityPerUSD).

entries.name: Cost-router (cheap)
entries.note: Darwin cost-router lands here: best quality-per-$ (5.36). Measured.

entries.name: Router v2 (escalating)
entries.note: escalation router; measured

entries.name: Opus-4 (frontier baseline)
entries.note: frontier baseline — barely beats cheap on quality at 10× cost. Measured.

entries.name: GPT-5 (frontier baseline)
entries.note: highest single-model quality; 19× the cost-router's price. Measured.

entries.name: Oracle (cost-optimal)
entries.note: upper bound: best achievable with perfect per-query routing
